title: englerodendron lebrunii
intro: Englerodendron lebrunii, also known as the 'African Teak', is a tropical evergreen tree native to central Africa. The medicinal properties of Englerodendron lebrunii have been found to contain bioactive compounds with anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which may contribute to its traditional use in treating various health conditions. The health benefits associated with the plant include the potential treatment of fever, rheumatism, and skin infections, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy. Englerodendron lebrunii is often used in traditional herbal preparations, such as infusions and decoctions, to harness its medicinal properties.
